case_name: Hachette Book Group v. Internet Archive
plaintiffs: Hachette Book Group / Penguin Random House / HarperCollins / Wiley
district_court_ruling: 2023-03-24 Judge John G. Koeltl rules for publishers; CDL not fair use; National Emergency Library + ordinary CDL practice both infringing
appeals_ruling: 2024-09-04 2nd Circuit affirms; all four fair use factors favor publishers; not transformative
status: Closed - IA did not petition for Supreme Court review by December 2024 deadline; permanent injunction limits IA's CDL to titles without commercial ebook editions; undisclosed monetary settlement to publishers (likely material to FY2023 net asset deficit -$3.53M)
key_disclosures: FY2023 990 expenses jump to $32.7M against $23.7M revenue (-$9.0M net income); FY2023 net assets fall to -$3.53M (publisher settlement landed here per Hachette legal observer commentary)

case_name: UMG Recordings v. Internet Archive (Great 78 Project)
plaintiffs: Universal Music Group / Sony Music / Concord / ABKCO
status: Settled 2025-09 via confidential resolution; case dismissal expected within 45 days of joint notice
key_disclosures: Initial ask $621M (Rolling Stone) / $412M (Silicon Republic); 2749 recordings at issue including Frank Sinatra Bing Crosby Chuck Berry Duke Ellington Ella Fitzgerald Louis Armstrong; settlement terms confidential
source: Rolling Stone / musictech.com / Bloomberg Law / blog.archive.org
